data.narrative:
Sateliot is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M), alongside a broader public comp set of 5 traded aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The three private comps span a wide range from $274M to $1.3B, with a median valuation of $400M. This median serves as the primary anchor. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because Sateliot's revenue is not disclosed, which is typical for private companies at this stage. The public sector median P/S stands at 49.8x across 6 tickers, but without target revenue data, this method yields no actionable result.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range reflects the tight clustering around the peer median, with $340M representing a modest discount and $460M a modest premium to that midpoint. The primary uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion in the peer set itself—the $1.026B spread between Sift and Aalyria—which suggests meaningful differentiation in traction, market position, or technology maturity. Revenue visibility and customer concentration metrics would materially improve conviction in any final valuation.
